title: JV-21 - Release candidate, rollback, and GA promotion gate for local MT default

labels.name: needs: human-reviewer
labels.description: Requires a bilingual or manual reviewer

labels.name: priority:P0
labels.description: Must-have for the first usable product increment

labels.name: level: atomic
labels.description: Small independent execution slice that can be assigned directly

body:
## Summary

Promote the local JA-VI MT default through RC to stable only after all evidence gates pass.

## Why is this needed?

Default replacement affects translation quality, privacy, packaging, and user trust. GA needs an explicit release gate and rollback plan.

## Current context

This WBS comes from tentacle research on 2026-05-21. Current repo state: mt_provider still defaults to google; src/bin/mt_bench.rs emits a pending lf-04-v1 fixture; local OPUS-MT provider exists behind local-mt but lacks benchmark-proven realtime replacement evidence; config has explicit mt_cloud_fallback consent; #372 closed with routing/fixture only; #383 tracks 60fps render performance; PR #407 aligns docs with local-first STT but does not make local MT default.

## Inputs

- Closed or accepted child issues JV-01..JV-20.
- Benchmark, soak, security, docs, packaging, release-smoke, updater evidence.
- Human/bilingual review if quality tradeoff is near threshold.

## Outputs

- Release candidate GitHub Release notes.
- Rollback plan to Google-default or previous version.
- GA promotion checklist and final evidence ledger.
- Post-release monitoring checklist.

## Implementation approach

- Publish pre-release first if any human acceptance or code-signing blocker remains.
- Require exact commit/tag evidence, not stale branch artifacts.
- Document known limitations and fallback instructions.
- Keep Google provider available as explicit user choice.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All mandatory gates pass or blockers are explicitly recorded.
- [ ] Release artifacts are generated from the merged commit/tag.
- [ ] Bilingual/human review signs off if automated quality is near threshold.
- [ ] Rollback path is tested and documented.
- [ ] Stable release notes clearly state local MT default behavior and fallback consent.

## Test cases / evidence gates

- [ ] CI status for tag.
- [ ] Release artifact checksum validation.
- [ ] Install/launch smoke on Windows.
- [ ] Benchmark and soak evidence linked.
- [ ] Updater detects RC/stable as configured.
- [ ] Final nfr/security/review verdicts CLEAN.

## Dependencies

- Depends on JV-01 through JV-20 or explicit deferrals.
- Depends on #383 successor evidence for 60fps.
- Blocks closing JV-EPIC.

## Agent routing

Orchestrator only for promotion; nfr-verification-gate, tui-security-auditor, tui-soak-monitor, and human reviewer where required.

## Project / WBS

- WBS key: JV-21
- Wave: RP4 GA
- Project target: magicpro97/project/2
- Labels: type: release, type: checkpoint, area: verification, area: packaging, needs: human-reviewer, priority:P0, level: atomic

## Duplicate / extension note

New release-promotion gate for local MT default.
